new_classes.label_en: fermented mash of malted barley with other cereal grains (grain whisky feedstock)
new_classes.label_de: vergorene Maische aus gemälzter Gerste mit anderen Getreidekornarten (Grain-Whisky-Ausgangsstoff)
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented mash consisting of malted barley together with whole grains of other cereals, used as the feedstock for grain whisky distillation.

A spirit, other than single malt Scotch whisky or blended malt Scotch whisky, produced at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash of malted barley with or without whole grains of other cereals.
A blend of two or more single grain whiskies from different distilleries.

new_classes.label_en: single grain whisky or blended grain whisky in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 30 69)
new_classes.label_de: Single-Grain-Whisky oder Blended-Grain-Whisky in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2208 30 69)
new_classes.definition_en: Single grain whisky or blended grain whisky presented in containers of a capacity exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 30 69.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria: (1) single grain whisky is produced at a single distillery (SingleDistilleryOriginQuality), (2) from a fermented mash of malted barley with or without other cereal grains (GrainMash feedstock), (3) with Scotch whisky origin (ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality), (4) with maturation quality, (5) blended grain whisky is a blend from multiple distilleries (MultiDistilleryOriginQuality + BlendingProcess), and (6) the specific CN 22083069 node is constrained to containers > 2 L (LargeContainer). The sibling node 22083061 (≤ 2 L) is already present in the TBox as ScotchWhiskyWithSoda22083061, but that class models a different product (Scotch whisky with soda water); the container volume distinction for grain/blended grain whisky pairs 22083061/22083069 is captured. Gaps: the legal text is truncated and does not provide the full definition of blended grain whisky; the exclusion of single malt and blended malt Scotch whisky from the grain whisky definition could ideally be expressed as complement restrictions, but this risks inconsistency without full disjointness axioms across the sibling hierarchy. Minimum alcohol strength and maturation period (3 years in oak, per Scotch Whisky Regulations) are implied but not in the provided snippet, so only partially covered via MaturationQuality.
